title: Optimization of contact angle and interfacial tension measurements for fluid/rock systems at ambient conditions
author: Alnoush, Sayed, et al.

steps_raw.title: Surface preparation
steps_raw.instruction: According to the above discussion, it is essential for rock-fluid wettability studies to be operated at conditions where the rock is well prepared (cleaned from impurities) and characterized. Studies on reservoir rocks (carbonates, sandstones and dolomite) or representative constituent rocks (such as calcite and quartz) are dependent on the wettability state and solid-fluid surface interactions. Thus, it is important to characterize and adjust the surface for optimized results. This is conducted through adjusting the surface roughness and treating it to remove contaminants as discussed in the following subsections.
